author: @totherarf
text:
I know you do a lot of "proof of concept" stuff, but .......

Why not, when you see something worthwhile, take a bit of time and make it nice and put the results on the web for sale?
After all ... what better message can you give than to let people show off your ideas (and get more people both aware of different approaches) and on to this channel? It could both fill the coffers (a bit) and give you an idea of how popular different Stuff is!

author: @happydrones9187
text:
Been trike fan for years..
My fav is 2front steering in the back..
Lever steering instead of a wheel..i.e. bobcat/skid-stearer..
Stretch the wheel base makes easy stearing..
Tip wheel backwards like a bike angle rake angle..same as a backwards bicycle..
Tipping is turning..
Hope its a neat twist..

text:
I am So looking forward to this!!
I am lucky enough to have bought a 2nd hand 'Bog - Standard' Assisted Pedal  E-bike...

But, it's very heavy, and I'm now needing either a sidecar....🐕‍🦺
Or an E-Trike!!

I shall be watching with Great Interest,
( and a little trepidation, Lol!!) 
* In my family, where I'm concerned ~It's a known Fact, that I must* "Keep it Simple, Stupid!" *or I, start to overcomplicate, anything I lay my hands on!🤦🏻‍♀️

I'm so grateful that I found your channel, and Hubby and I, love the positive joy that you bring to your projects!!❤️

* In fact, if you were able to somehow put Your Energy into a battery, then the Energy Crisis would be over, and, with No Detriment to the Earth's Ecology!!🤔😊
I hope this comment finds you well, and, if there's a race, my money's on you!! 😁

author: @taranof
text:
I drive a trike, Hase Kettwiesel (one front wheel, two back). Very nice.
One suggestion for you: why not build something like the Banana Peel Trike? Power that rear axle you got, and steer by leaning left and right. You could probably reuse parts of the paper bike  for the front wheel assembly !

author: @trplankowner71
text: The difference between leading dual wheels and trailing is a choice between maximizing stability or traction. For a low weight electric trike, traction may be preferred. But then again we are taking about England, where the question is not if it rained today,  buy how much.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: lol - true enough but then again - I do like the traditional delta frame

replies.author: @another3997
replies.text: Delta trikes usually only have one drive wheel, whilst the other simply rotates freely. Some trikes have a differential, but then, you can still end up with only one wheel drive... on the wheel with the least grip. Delta trikes aren't necessarily less stable than a tadpole trike. It depends on the wheelbase, width, centre of gravity and overall geometry.

replies.author: @trplankowner71
replies.text: Well, if one doesn't need the stability, you can use 2 hub motor wheels in the rear. Construction will be much easier and there will be much more power for acceleration and carrying a heavier load. Of course you won't be able to use less expensive parts like Robert does in his project here. Which is why I love Robert's channel so much. Anyone can build a project if they have an extravagant budget.  A big budget is like brute force, if you have enough of it, you can do almost anything. Paraphrasing another one of my favorite YouTubers, Isaac Arthur.
